issues.description: The script uses GM_xmlhttpRequest and GM_download which can send network requests potentially to third-party servers. The updateURL and downloadURL point to greasyfork.org, a known script hosting site. No evidence found of user data being sent to unknown third-party servers.
issues.recommendation: Ensure that network requests are only made to trusted domains and no sensitive user data is transmitted without explicit user consent.

issues.description: The script accesses GM_setValue, GM_getValue, GM_deleteValue which may store user preferences locally but no indication of reading sensitive cookies or storage data.
issues.recommendation: Avoid accessing or transmitting sensitive user data such as cookies or form inputs unless necessary and with user consent.

issues.description: The script does not use eval(), new Function(), or dynamic script injection from remote sources. No @require directives found that load remote scripts.
issues.recommendation: Maintain no use of remote code execution functions to prevent injection attacks.

issues.description: The script requests multiple GM_* permissions including GM_xmlhttpRequest, GM_download, GM_setClipboard, GM_addElement, unsafeWindow. These are consistent with the script's functionality (downloading videos, clipboard access, UI enhancements). No unused high permissions detected.
issues.recommendation: Review permissions periodically to ensure no excessive privileges are requested.

issues.description: No usage of sensitive APIs like navigator.geolocation, RTCPeerConnection, MediaDevices, or Clipboard API detected beyond GM_setClipboard which is granted explicitly.
issues.recommendation: Monitor usage of sensitive APIs to avoid privacy leaks.

issues.description: No signs of code obfuscation or base64 decoding for execution found in the provided code snippet.
issues.recommendation: Keep code readable and avoid obfuscation to facilitate security audits.

issues.description: The external dependencies (updateURL and downloadURL) point to greasyfork.org, a reputable open-source script repository. No @require directives found, so no external JS libraries loaded.
issues.recommendation: Use fixed versions and trusted sources for any external dependencies to prevent supply chain attacks.

details: No evidence of reading document.cookie, localStorage, or sessionStorage.
No event listeners for keyboard input events detected.
No access to form input values or browser fingerprinting APIs detected.

summary: The script requests multiple permissions consistent with its functionality, including network requests and clipboard access. Network requests are made only to known trusted domains (greasyfork.org) for updates and downloads. No evidence of user data exfiltration, privacy-invasive data collection, or remote code execution was found. No excessive permissions or code obfuscation detected. Overall, the script appears low risk but users should remain cautious about permissions granted.
